test_scenario: User provides ISSUE=42 with conflicting user-typed WORK_ITEM; GitHub issue body describes a CSV import bug
skill_specification_references: SKILL.md:29 (Non-negotiable rule #1: GitHub issue title/body is authoritative)
references/workflow.md:52 (Phase 1 Step 1: infer WORK_ITEM from issue, treat user text as supplemental)
result_summary: The skill correctly specifies that when ISSUE is provided, the GitHub issue title/body is the authoritative WORK_ITEM source. User-typed WORK_ITEM text is treated as supplemental context only. The supplied issue (#42) remains the parent issue and a new parent issue should not be created.
notes: This is the core principle that allows coding-task-workflow to remain grounded in persistent GitHub state rather than transient user input. The specification explicitly states this rule three times across different documents (SKILL.md rule #1, workflow.md Phase 1 step 1, and Workflow-rule answers), emphasizing its importance.
